---
[](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/101750508/526948178/98320660?source=SBD_VF_EM_FR_020625_1_1_VotingRights&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMzMvMS85NTc5MyIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICIxNTU2ZjJmMC1hNWU1LWVmMTEtOTBjYi0wMDIyNDgyYTk0ZjQiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=1lMhKO2V8Guhl9L-A7RBeoIwByIjEbwvHLLa7-6O_G0=&emci=4700773d-e0e3-ef11-90cb-0022482a94f4&emdi=1556f2f0-a5e5-ef11-90cb-0022482a94f4&ceid=799895) Peter, earlier this week a lawsuit related to the gerrymandered NC Senate maps headed to trial.

**This year will mark the 60th anniversary of the Voting Rights Act. But, we are still seeing attempts to silence Black and Brown communities and limit their representation.**

From manipulating our voting maps to dilute the voting power of communities of color, rolling back mail-in and early voting options often utilized by many voters of color, and weaponizing incarceration status to permanently deny voting rights based on criminal convictions — **Americans are still facing unethical voter suppression tactics that aim to silence voices at the ballot box and beyond.**

I’ve said it once, and I’ll continue to say it again and again: **Voting is a fundamental right of citizenship, and every American should have an equal voice in our democracy.**

I’m in Congress fighting for a country where democracy can flourish and where the fundamental right to vote is protected for all Americans. [But Peter, democracy is nothing without teamwork. Will you add your name to stand with me?](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/101750514/526948183/98320660?source=SBD_VF_EM_FR_020625_1_1_VotingRights&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMzMvMS85NTc5MyIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICIxNTU2ZjJmMC1hNWU1LWVmMTEtOTBjYi0wMDIyNDgyYTk0ZjQiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=1lMhKO2V8Guhl9L-A7RBeoIwByIjEbwvHLLa7-6O_G0=&emci=4700773d-e0e3-ef11-90cb-0022482a94f4&emdi=1556f2f0-a5e5-ef11-90cb-0022482a94f4&ceid=799895) | [SIGN ON](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/101750520/526948189/98320660?source=SBD_VF_EM_FR_020625_1_1_VotingRights&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMzMvMS85NTc5MyIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICIxNTU2ZjJmMC1hNWU1LWVmMTEtOTBjYi0wMDIyNDgyYTk0ZjQiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=1lMhKO2V8Guhl9L-A7RBeoIwByIjEbwvHLLa7-6O_G0=&emci=4700773d-e0e3-ef11-90cb-0022482a94f4&emdi=1556f2f0-a5e5-ef11-90cb-0022482a94f4&ceid=799895)
---
I’ve witnessed North Carolina evolve from a segregated state to a state embracing its diverse communities. **But, I know progress takes time — and I am committing all of mine to ensure everyone has a seat, and a say, at the table.**

We must remember that our elections should reflect the will of the people, not the will of politicians. The stakes have never been higher, Peter.

Thank you,

Valerie Foushee

clean_body: Earlier this week a lawsuit related to the gerrymandered NC Senate maps headed to trial. If you’d like to unsubscribe, | | = Peter, earlier this week a lawsuit related to the gerrymandered NC Senate maps headed to trial. This year will mark the 60th anniversary of the Voting Rights Act. But, we are still seeing attempts to silence Black and Brown communities and limit their representation. From manipulating our voting maps to dilute the voting power of communities of color, rolling back mail-in and early voting options often utilized by many voters of color, and weaponizing incarceration status to permanently deny voting rights based on criminal convictions — Americans are still facing unethical voter suppression tactics that aim to silence voices at the ballot box and beyond. I’ve said it once, and I’ll continue to say it again and again: Voting is a fundamental right of citizenship, and every American should have an equal voice in our democracy. I’m in Congress fighting for a country where democracy can flourish and where the fundamental right to vote is protected for all Americans. | I’ve witnessed North Carolina evolve from a segregated state to a state embracing its diverse communities. But, I know progress takes time — and I am committing all of mine to ensure everyone has a seat, and a say, at the table. We must remember that our elections should reflect the will of the people, not the will of politicians. The stakes have never been higher, Peter. Thank you, Valerie Foushee * | | | ---|---|--- = In Congress, Valerie Foushee will be a champion for working families, a leader to reform our criminal justice system and tackle systemic racism, and a fighter to protect our environment and address climate change. | = = Paid for by Foushee for Congress Valerie Foushee for Congress PO Box 16446 Chapel Hill, NC 27516 United States If you need to you can unsubscribe, You can also =.

Brad's storied career as a prosecutor and Wisconsin's Attorney General proves that he's tough on criminals, supports law enforcement, and defends victims. Brad is a constitutionalist. He loves our state, and I believe he will make a remarkable Supreme Court Justice. Get off the bench – from defending our Constitution to upholding the rule of law and protecting our kids – there is too much at stake. Sincerely, Glenn Grothman | | | | | | | FOLLOW GLENN ON SOCIAL MEDIA! | | | | ---|---|---|--- Glenn Grothman for Congress P.O. name: Beto O'Rourke
subject: Goya and Gaudí or: the case for hope
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Powered by People, [poweredxpeople
body:
#
Amy and I took the kids to Spain for a week just after Christmas.
It turned out to be just what we needed, even if we didn’t know it when we planned the trip. The distance from home gave us some needed perspective; the concentrated time together helped us get back in tune amid the rush of our kids’ teenage lives, especially as our oldest heads off to college next year; and just seeing another country and culture, taking in its customs (and food!) was really wonderful. We are lucky.
Two things really stayed with me from that trip...
**[If you are interested in reading more on this, you can click here to read my new post on Substack.](https://act.betoorourke.com/go/63893?t=1&akid=239970%2E444211%2EU-r_NQ)**
More soon,
Beto

**[Donate](https://secure.actblue.com/donate/pbp-em-2023-footer?refcode=em20250207-239970-footer&t=2&refcode2=239970_444211_U-r_NQ&akid=239970%2E444211%2EU-r_NQ)**

name: JusticeDemocrats.com
subject: The Democratic Party needs to fight harder
disclaimer_text: PAID FOR BY JUSTICE DEMOCRATS
body:
[1]Justice Democrats

Peter,

Donald Trump and billionaire Elon Musk are attempting a corporate coup on
our federal government while unconstitutionally attacking our fundamental
rights — and the Democratic Party so far has largely failed to meet the
moment.

Everyday people have noticed and started pushing back, calling out
Democrats to do their jobs. Thousands protested the Trump administration’s
attempt to shut down USAID in Washington, D.C. this week. When Democratic
leaders addressed the crowds, people shouted, “We know! Do something about
it!”

Now, more and more Democratic senators are pledging to block Trump’s
nominees until Musk’s hostile takeover is stopped. It’s about time!

But Justice Democrats leaders have been outspoken and fiercely fighting
back since Day 1. Reps. Rashida Tlaib and Delia Ramirez joined protestors
outside of the Department of Labor — Musk and DOGE’s next target — to show
solidarity and urge their fellow Democrats in the House and Senate to
fight this threat with the strength and urgency it demands.

[ https://secure.actblue.com/donate/jd-em-20250207?refcode=em20250207-36411&amounts=10%2C27%2C50%2C100%2C250%2C500%2C1000&amount=5&refcode2=36411_1203867_a4bRyJ&akid=36411%2E1203867%2Ea4bRyJ ]A screenshot of two posts on X. The first is from Congresswoman Rashida
Tlaib reading: 'We protested at the U.S. Department of Labor, where an
unelected billionaire is dismantling worker protections and pretending
it's about efficiency. We have to fight in Congress, the courts, and in
the streets.' The post includes 3 pictures of protesters outside the
Department of Labor. The second post is from Congresswoman Delia C.
Ramirez reading: 'Musk is targeting the Dept. of Labor. We can't act like
it's business as usual. I will not concede the power of the people.' The
post includes a screenshot of a video of the Congresswoman with the
caption ''Do something.' 'What are you'.

[ https://secure.actblue.com/donate/jd-em-20250207?refcode=em20250207-36411&amounts=10%2C27%2C50%2C100%2C250%2C500%2C1000&amount=5&refcode2=36411_1203867_a4bRyJ&akid=36411%2E1203867%2Ea4bRyJ ]Clearly, we need more of that bold leadership in Congress. Help us
recruit and elect more working-class progressives to join Rashida, Delia,
and the rest of our Justice Democrats bloc in fiercely fighting for the
people: pitch in $5 now!

[ https://secure.actblue.com/donate/jd-em-20250207?refcode=em20250207-36411&amounts=10%2C27%2C50%2C100%2C250%2C500%2C1000&amount=5&refcode2=36411_1203867_a4bRyJ&akid=36411%2E1203867%2Ea4bRyJ ]Contribute $5

In solidarity,

Justice Democrats

Contribute to Justice Democrats: https://secure.actblue.com/donate/justicedemocrats?refcode=em20250207-36411-footer&t=1002&refcode2=36411_1203867_a4bRyJ&akid=36411%2E1203867%2Ea4bRyJ

name: Rushern Baker III
subject: We're running out of time—deadline is Tonight!

We need **200 people to chip in $25 today** to stay on track. Every dollar makes a difference, and your support right now will help us push forward when it matters most. | | **SEN. KATIE BRITT: Laken Riley Act our first step in ending era of open borders – here's what's next**
---
[](http://links.katiebrittforsenate.com/u/click?_t=64058844820c43dab0e6ed55204bc487&_m=8865ed1e494443a78ce828c8af07ae8b&_e=yfUTfIC70bZ_Ru8ziLW2FwdHKOwJNbNVmmwxj-S_2xPCE-ghnlD141_J908EdUvoiIdtpSdH5Z-g5c0MUsl-Ane0SILINwaethUYWSzSr4NbPBNrbb2oB4OmD7d-hiHn07jte5UxWvTEdhDPi27z4dcwi4XDYFDlbc9tt1YCpoJANiVrnwjU8HrKPSHbwm_ASbMEPpBdc76H5S8nqo4RzPXNcPpDCzyLxAemVoI60l_keMxTXPTvqKJ9-XU94AjeJkXY3SxQMTYd0OMaYP5jVgwZS5uOgexuCB4klH3tb4sCX_4Skh6PnvwVXf6Xdl_a)
---
When President Donald Trump signed the Laken Riley Act into law last Wednesday, it marked a new beginning for the United States – a new Golden Age of America, as President Trump put it in his inaugural address. In signing what was the most significant piece of immigration-enforcement legislation to reach the Resolute Desk since 1996, President Trump signaled that the era of open borders is over.
---

| | **Sen. Britt announces Senate Judiciary Subcommittee assignments**
---
[](http://links.katiebrittforsenate.com/u/click?_t=64058844820c43dab0e6ed55204bc487&_m=8865ed1e494443a78ce828c8af07ae8b&_e=yfUTfIC70bZ_Ru8ziLW2F7Scoz7n9zgBOTpDZy5w0-8NqFaPtt9wdH0DhkJIkS5xfG5cSk-ug4vXgrOhb2O90C5IH-Oz-kMRM8k8lNE-lFLmSuGnfYZb784t9T7hnYZ8379BsC24U6RgAyxZ4CVFCb8fW3tijH5gNmwtfBRN6cDdKv_9X7lGegowcQYlKm0m8Z-YfCGe3pmHqAmIUubtnXejUjLZ17xENtbcQ6oKpGxBEvoWlqvnM1EaQavc8-ZiSe1rduIfiiU5hz6JZ17VQBxRTojLLfo9v9viOmDsqkQ8x54ewqQ-ckP2Uga4-Nak)
---
U.S. Senator Katie Britt, R-Ala., Wednesday announced her subcommittee assignments on the Senate Judiciary Committee in the 119th Congress. “I am honored to join the Border Security and Immigration and the Crime and Counterterrorism Subcommittees. Both of these subcommittees will play a pivotal role in making America safer. I’m looking forward to working with my colleagues to build on the success of the Laken Riley Act with additional pieces of legislation to strengthen border security and interior immigration enforcement, including reducing the flow of deadly fentanyl into our country. I am committed to fighting for America First policies to secure and defend our homeland while giving our law enforcement officers the tools and resources to combat threats,” said Senator Britt.
---

| | **Let’s expand K-12 education freedom to children in every state**
---
[](http://links.katiebrittforsenate.com/u/click?_t=64058844820c43dab0e6ed55204bc487&_m=8865ed1e494443a78ce828c8af07ae8b&_e=yfUTfIC70bZ_Ru8ziLW2F9VDyczSBoQnSYY9gcAX-LiIXsQX3PtsiiWSI-BaLz9KbhZ0cHENFpb3dyxK5ayK03MNCrJjFFQvdbVTf3c9OZQI2oSYFIi9gglByuuQvwqeUGe7x0mFLB_4cUd0s_9_oa00HVs4EGkMYI0JNDMNbpmtUuPO1C32nHZjGOGEh1w-28uqplwNAc2BaUstMqa-SJ7qVnC2ZMknnJIq--VX0Vv-Nb5--Ae8_696dspk3no_)
---
My perspective as a mom of two school-aged kids informs nearly everything I do as a United States senator, and it is through my own life and conversations with other parents that I came to understand the importance of achieving universal educational choice as an option for every student in America. That is why, during this year’s National School Choice Week, I once again proudly joined as a co-sponsor of the Educational Choice for Children Act, introduced by Senate Committee on Health, Education, Labor and Pensions Chairman Bill Cassidy. This bill would empower millions of parents in Alabama and across the country to access high-quality education that suits their child’s unique needs, regardless of income or zip code.
---

clean_body: | | | | | Official Team Britt Newsletter | | SEN. KATIE BRITT: Laken Riley Act our first step in ending era of open borders – here's what's next When President Donald Trump signed the Laken Riley Act into law last Wednesday, it marked a new beginning for the United States – a new Golden Age of America, as President Trump put it in his inaugural address. In signing what was the most significant piece of immigration-enforcement legislation to reach the Resolute Desk since 1996, President Trump signaled that the era of open borders is over. | | Sen. Britt announces Senate Judiciary Subcommittee assignments U.S. Senator Katie Britt, R-Ala., Wednesday announced her subcommittee assignments on the Senate Judiciary Committee in the 119th Congress. “I am honored to join the Border Security and Immigration and the Crime and Counterterrorism Subcommittees. Both of these subcommittees will play a pivotal role in making America safer. I’m looking forward to working with my colleagues to build on the success of the Laken Riley Act with additional pieces of legislation to strengthen border security and interior immigration enforcement, including reducing the flow of deadly fentanyl into our country. I am committed to fighting for America First policies to secure and defend our homeland while giving our law enforcement officers the tools and resources to combat threats,” said Senator Britt. | | Let’s expand K-12 education freedom to children in every state My perspective as a mom of two school-aged kids informs nearly everything I do as a United States senator, and it is through my own life and conversations with other parents that I came to understand the importance of achieving universal educational choice as an option for every student in America. That is why, during this year’s National School Choice Week, I once again proudly joined as a co-sponsor of the Educational Choice for Children Act, introduced by Senate Committee on Health, Education, Labor and Pensions Chairman Bill Cassidy. This bill would empower millions of parents in Alabama and across the country to access high-quality education that suits their child’s unique needs, regardless of income or zip code. | | | | | | | | | Alabama First Coffee Mug Alabama values. Alabama first. name: Elizabeth Warren (via Democrats.org)
subject: Project 2025
body:
---
Derek, During the 2024 election cycle, the American people became familiar with Project 2025. This document laid out Republicans’ plans to reshape our country if they gained control. The plans in that document are the stuff of nightmares: firing workers without cause, targeting political opponents, ripping hard-earned retirement and health care benefits away from seniors, dismantling public education, and funding tax cuts for the rich by raising taxes on everyone else. Over and over again, Donald Trump swore he had nothing to do with those plans. On the campaign trail, he said, “I know nothing about Project 2025.” He promised, “I have nothing to do with Project 2025.” He lied. Shortly after the election, Trump nominated one of Project 2025’s chief architects — Russ Vought — to oversee the government’s entire budget office, and attempt to make that nightmare a reality. So earlier this week, my fellow Senate Democrats and I held the floor to protest Vought’s nomination. **Americans voted each of us into office to fight for them. They do not expect us to roll over and play dead.** Even if we’re in the minority, we can slow and limit Trump’s destruction. With every fight, we can build political power to put more checks on his administration and build the foundation for future wins. I understand my assignment in the Senate, and I’m asking you to get involved too, Derek. Whether it’s running for office yourself, supporting a neighbor’s campaign, or getting involved in an organization taking action, we all have to continue to make investments in our democracy. [**One of the best ways to do that is by making a $3 contribution to the DNC as they work to elect as many Democrats as possible who will check Trump’s power. Anything you can contribute today will go a very long way as we work to strengthen our Party:**](https://secure.actblue.com/donate/em-dnc-february-2025?amount=3&refcode=em_20250207_2737_fr&refcode2=2748_22930333_wvPQbS&akid=2748%2E22930333%2EwvPQbS) _If you 've stored your info with ActBlue, we'll process your contribution instantly._ | | | **[Donate $3](https://secure.actblue.com/donate/em-dnc-february-2025?express_lane=true&amount=3&refcode=em_20250207_2737_fr&refcode2=2748_22930333_wvPQbS&akid=2748%2E22930333%2EwvPQbS)**

Thanks for being a part of this,
Elizabeth Warren

name: Elise Stefanik
subject: This message is from the heart.
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Team Elise, a joint fundraising committee authorized by and composed of Elise for Congress and E-PAC
body:
Thank you so much for everything.
| **__A Personal Note from the Desk of Rep. Elise Stefanik__**
---
Peter,
---
Thousands of my constituents and supporters hosted events to thank me for more than a decade of fighting for hard-working families in New York’s 21st Congressional District over the weekend.
---
To call it inspiring would be an understatement.
---
**I was completely blown away by the outpouring of support from so many Americans who’ve had my back for so long and done so much to help power Republicans to victory last November.**
---
And while I appreciated the gesture, _YOU_ deserve just as much credit as I do, Peter.
---
Our historic victory wouldn’t have been possible without your unwavering support!
---
But unfortunately, the fight for America’s future is far from over.
---
**As I continue fighting to Make America Great Again, I want to ask you one more time to stand with me by making a grassroots contribution of _ANY_ amount to defend our House majority. **
---
_We fought so hard to help America First Republicans win the governing trifecta of the House, the Senate, and the White House. Now, we’re counting on your sustained support to defend it._
---
_(I know you’ve already done a lot, so please don’t feel pressured to contribute if you’ve already done all that you can – or if you simply aren’t in the position to do so.)_
---
[**Otherwise, please consider making a grassroots contribution of ANY amount to help defend our House majority.**](http://links.conservativeum.com/s/c/GdbtUDfRBy9FF_qrJOLt9Pj38K3IMbkBy6ImKidQKDDHhqi3S7swFJKCxnW7ekMrr1GOUJ2UUtnaMon2DI9j44aqin44iNOQJr24PpbwfupAd2fvasSiLa1P8-wARYbmodiOY16IBK4sE_YmvvX7dRys6euFHyxG2qKIjxQvKKBRxDZ-PdMIBULFa0A8LziZMRkRAcshiBqGm8QTHHOOGhDrpFSkTHcVNh9c555ax1g-rFYoD80VVo8RL1t4LNoCNoh1uBPDCE6fLDs_lhHOzTV6vckNlI6jr2JABWQziAFPPfiq0WcBYXh5ALdhY7FnkOmfRN3aMn3fryaxbiYVYZq-5uarp2p3naI2RySm7pU4hqWhZ0-9vm7UqLVrPMIpq3AgnM-7HtdbBqQWMKTcBhlNLdY2xZNDmwxTanBjIAJmy7ojE4_aigbLs7AOmPXqHDwM5joRnb18N4glaikADvChroEbzbWe4BYEI4P7rOTgFO3P/3KGheQFkbGIo933yIdajFfb7DiagrCvL/5)

clean_body: Thank you so much for everything. | A Personal Note from the Desk of Rep. Elise Stefanik Peter, Thousands of my constituents and supporters hosted events to thank me for more than a decade of fighting for hard-working families in New York’s 21st Congressional District over the weekend. To call it inspiring would be an understatement. I was completely blown away by the outpouring of support from so many Americans who’ve had my back for so long and done so much to help power Republicans to victory last November. And while I appreciated the gesture, YOU deserve just as much credit as I do, Peter. Our historic victory wouldn’t have been possible without your unwavering support! But unfortunately, the fight for America’s future is far from over. As I continue fighting to Make America Great Again, I want to ask you one more time to stand with me by making a grassroots contribution of ANY amount to defend our House majority. We fought so hard to help America First Republicans win the governing trifecta of the House, the Senate, and the White House. Now, we’re counting on your sustained support to defend it. (I know you’ve already done a lot, so please don’t feel pressured to contribute if you’ve already done all that you can – or if you simply aren’t in the position to do so.) Thank you and God bless, Rep. Elise Stefanik Paid for by Team Elise, a joint fundraising committee authorized by and composed of Elise for Congress and E-PAC. www.elisestefanik.com PO Box 500 Glens Falls, NY 12801 |

name: Dawn Moore
subject: A wife, a mother, and the First Lady of Maryland
body:
[](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/101752769/526954340/-926250309?nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMzAvMS85MDQ1NyIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICJjZjQyNGMxMC1hZmU1LWVmMTEtOTBjYi0wMDIyNDgyYTk0ZjQiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=IF4xup43ZBIpXRRq2ah_nfiQwDX-tJMwh-yc4dva09Q=&emci=4622797b-b5e4-ef11-90cb-0022482a94f4&emdi=cf424c10-afe5-ef11-90cb-0022482a94f4&ceid=24635485)
Friend, it’s Dawn. If you didn’t already know me, I am a wife, a mother, and the First Lady of Maryland. **The governor’s house is the people’s house.** It is my honor to be living here, and to serve the people of Maryland. With over 20 years of leadership experience in government and politics, I am passionate about doing my part to create a state that works for all Marylanders. Right now, we are facing a budget crisis that we are working hard to make sure is resolved in a way that benefits all Marylanders. Wes has proposed a bold plan to meet the moment. This plan gives nearly two-thirds of Marylanders a tax cut, will lower the corporate tax rate and focus on making Maryland more competitive and business-friendly, and cuts spending by $2 billion. It is a bold plan, made to meet this moment which requires bold action. If we want to keep investing in our future, we need a sustainable budget — which is exactly what Wes has proposed. Wes is making the tough decisions now so that we can continue to pave the way for Marylanders to find good jobs, earn fair wages, and have opportunities to increase wealth. By creating jobs, prioritizing regulatory reform, and making it easier to grow and stay in Maryland, Wes will be fighting to ensure that the future is bright for this state. I will do whatever I can in my power to help push forward this plan so that Maryland can win this decade. **We lead by partnering together, leaving no one behind.** With gratitude, Dawn Flythe Moore
First Lady of Maryland
---

It is my honor to be living here, and to serve the people of Maryland. With over 20 years of leadership experience in government and politics, I am passionate about doing my part to create a state that works for all Marylanders. Right now, we are facing a budget crisis that we are working hard to make sure is resolved in a way that benefits all Marylanders. Wes has proposed a bold plan to meet the moment. This plan gives nearly two-thirds of Marylanders a tax cut, will lower the corporate tax rate and focus on making Maryland more competitive and business-friendly, and cuts spending by $2 billion. It is a bold plan, made to meet this moment which requires bold action. If we want to keep investing in our future, we need a sustainable budget — which is exactly what Wes has proposed. Wes is making the tough decisions now so that we can continue to pave the way for Marylanders to find good jobs, earn fair wages, and have opportunities to increase wealth.
